methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Nu Quantum, a Series A quantum computing company founded by Carmen Palacios-Berraquero, has raised $60M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comps (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The stage-progression method was applied, using the $60M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $120M. The peer-median method was not applicable: while 7 same-sector peers exist, the filter for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers produced insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3), making a narrow peer median unreliable. A broader all-sector median would introduce excessive noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median estimate of $120M. This range reflects a single triangulation method, introducing material uncertainty around point estimates. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this stage transition in quantum computing specifically—a sector with heterogeneous business models and development timelines—and the absence of direct Series A peer valuation data to ground expectations. Additional uncertainty stems from the nascent market for quantum hardware and services, where comparable public multiples may not fully capture private risk premiums.
